third_place_rules: The 12 third-placed teams (one per group A-L) are ranked in a single comparison table; the top 8 advance to the Round of 32. Ranking criteria in order: 1) points; 2) goal difference; 3) goals scored; 4) highest team conduct score (fair play: yellow -1, indirect red -3, direct red -4, yellow+direct red -5); 5) position in the latest FIFA World Ranking (then earlier rankings per some sources; drawing of lots only as theoretical last resort - this final step wording unverified against the raw FIFA regulations PDF). Allocation: the 8 qualified third-placed teams fill the away slots of 8 fixed Round-of-32 matches against group winners - Match 74 (W-E vs 3rd A/B/C/D/F), 77 (W-I vs 3rd C/D/F/G/H), 79 (W-A vs 3rd C/E/F/H/I), 80 (W-L vs 3rd E/H/I/J/K), 81 (W-D vs 3rd B/E/F/I/J), 82 (W-G vs 3rd A/E/H/I/J), 85 (W-B vs 3rd E/F/G/I/J), 87 (W-K vs 3rd D/E/I/J/L). The exact assignment depends on WHICH 8 of the 12 groups supply qualifying third-placed teams: FIFA's regulations contain an annex table covering all 495 possible combinations (C(12,8)), each mapping the qualified thirds to specific match slots so that no team meets an opponent from its own group in the Round of 32 and bracket balance is preserved.
notes: All 32 knockout fixtures cross-verified across 2 full independent sources (Wikipedia knockout-stage page; worldcuppass.com) plus spot-checks (FOX Sports, NBC/ESPN search results, worldcuplocaltime). Full agreement on match numbers, dates, pairings, venues; one source's "AT&T Toronto" for Match 83 is a typo - Toronto venue is BMO Field. FIFA.com pages returned empty/404 via fetch, so FIFA's own site could not be read directly; FIFA stadium branding differs for sponsor names (SoFi = Los Angeles Stadium, Estadio BBVA = Estadio Monterrey, Levi's = San Francisco Bay Area Stadium, Estadio Azteca = Estadio Ciudad de Mexico, AT&T = Dallas Stadium, etc.). Kickoff for the final: 2026-07-19, 15:00 ET at MetLife.
